title: Comparison-view layout rebalance
summary: Eighteenth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The first shared-species comparison pass landed with sound unlock logic and good ecological direction, but the live comparison-open view is too vertically tight inside the `192x144` journal shell. This follow-up should keep the same-pane comparison concept while rebalancing the open view so the comparison cards become the clearest part of the pane instead of the most cramped.

execution_order.goal: Rebalance the comparison-open journal layout so the habitat cards stay readable at `192x144`.
execution_order.why_now: The feature concept is worth keeping, but the current open state is too cramped in the live notebook shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the comparison-layout follow-up for readability and notebook fit.
execution_order.why_now: This is a visual polish fix on a high-leverage teaching surface, so it should get one clean critique pass before other journal-depth work builds on it.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
Keep the feature same-pane and spoiler-safe.
Do not expand the candidate set while fixing layout readability.
Prefer giving the comparison cards more room over preserving every non-comparison detail line in the open view.
Keep the notebook calm; do not solve this by turning comparison mode into a new full-screen surface.
acceptance_focus: Comparison cards are readable in the live `192x144` notebook shell.
The comparison-open view still feels compact and notebook-like.
The same spoiler-safe, note-backed comparison model remains intact.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-shared-species-comparison-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-shared-species-comparison-review.md, and this packet. Keep the same-pane comparison feature, but rebalance the open comparison layout so the habitat cards become clearly readable at `192x144` without widening scope.
